channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - kotaku.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for kotaku.com

item.title: Harada, Not Like This…
item.content:encoded:
The Tekken director is part of a new studio that would have sounded cool like, a decade ago

Back in December, Tekken director Katsuhiro Harada announced he was leaving Bandai Namco after decades of leading the publisher’s marquee fighting game series. His announcement was reflective and heartfelt, and fans wished him well wherever he was going next. Now, we know where he’s going, and oh, no…not like this, Harada.

Harada is now the CEO and director at VS Studio, a new team under the SNK umbrella. At a glance, that sounds like the stuff of fanfiction, with one veteran fighting game legend heading over to another company known for classics like King of Fighters and Fatal Fury, and in theory, this could be a really impactful studio for moving the genre forward. However, look a little closer at the state of SNK, and the cynical cracks start to set in. 

Electronic Gaming Development Company, a company owned by the Saudi Arabian prime minister, Mohammed bin Salman, acquired SNK back in 2022 as part of what appears to be a concentrated effort on the part of the Saudi Arabian government to bring its “sportswashing” campaign to gaming. The country with tons of money but a very checkered record on human rights has set about acquiring stakes in developers and publishers like EA and Capcom, as well as taking over long-standing cultural events like the EVO fighting game tournament.

Harada opening a new studio should be exciting, but now it’s got a giant asterisk next to it. It remains to be seen whether or not VS Studio SNK suffers under its parent company, though Fatal Fury has made weird choices since the acquisition, from using AI slop to including soccer player Cristiano Ronaldo as a playable character for no discernible reason.



https://kotaku.com/katsuhiro-harada-tekken-vs-studio-snk-saudi-arabia-2000695342
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: One GTA 6 Fan Is So Obsessed They’re Tracking Foot Traffic At A Café Near Rockstar To Predict The Next Trailer Drop
item.content:encoded:
The last trailer for Grand Theft Auto 6 hit the internet over a year ago. Since then, fans have been waiting for more information and another big look at the upcoming open-world game. Many were convinced that the third trailer would arrive on May 12. And one dedicated fan decided to look at how busy a nearby tea café is via Google to try and prove that maybe, just maybe, the trailer is coming soon.

For various reasons that aren’t really important, a large number of overly online GTA fans have convinced themselves that this was the week that Rockstar would release GTA 6‘s third trailer. And maybe that will happen. I really hope so, because the longer we go without a new GTA 6 trailer, the more wild and out of control some fans get while waiting. We’ve seen them monitor moon cycles, use math to figure out what SPF sunscreen an NPC in a trailer was using, and even fly to Rockstar’s office to harass devs with questions about the upcoming game. Now, perhaps inspired by the Pentagon Pizza Theory, a fan is tracking foot traffic at a café a block away from Rockstar North’s studio via Google and sharing it online.

On May 12, Reddit user ak47rocks1337yt posted on the GTA 6 subreddit a screenshot from Google Maps showing that a cafe near the GTA studio’s main office was busier than usual. “Could this be it?” asked the user, in reference to the idea that May 12 could be the day trailer three goes live. The idea, I think, is that GTA 6 devs are free to grab tea and celebrate ahead of the trailer’s release.



https://kotaku.com/one-gta-6-fan-is-so-obsessed-theyre-tracking-foot-traffic-at-a-cafe-near-rockstar-to-predict-the-next-trailer-drop-2000695325
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
